data.headline: Bathroom Remodeling in Westport, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut bathroom remodeling contractor serving Westport and Fairfield County , complete renovations from walk-in showers to full master suites.

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling in Westport, CT
data.content:
Westport homeowners are design-conscious and expect bathrooms that reflect current trends while remaining functional for families. Homes in Compo Beach, Greens Farms, and Saugatuck have bathrooms ranging from dated 1970s layouts with tub-shower combos to larger but aging 1990s master baths with dated tile and fixtures.

BuiltWell provides full-service bathroom remodeling in Westport , from compact hall bath updates to complete master suite renovations. We handle every trade in-house: tile, plumbing, electrical, carpentry, and painting.

data.title: What Is Included in a Bathroom Remodel in Westport
data.content:
A bathroom remodel in Westport covers demolition, waterproofing, tile, plumbing fixtures, vanity, electrical, ventilation, and all Connecticut permits, managed as one project from initial planning through final walkthrough. Westport homes near the coast and along the Saugatuck River corridor tend to have larger master bathrooms where the finish expectations are high and the details, grout joint consistency, threshold alignment, fixture centerlines, are examined closely at completion.

We begin with full demolition of all tile, fixtures, vanity, and flooring, then assess the subfloor, wall framing, and existing plumbing rough-in before materials are selected. Westport homes at lower elevations near the water sometimes show subfloor moisture damage from seasonal humidity that was never addressed by the original exhaust fan, and we replace compromised framing and confirm the full vent path exits to the exterior as part of the rough-in phase.

Waterproofing is installed behind all shower and tub areas using membrane systems, with cement board used throughout wet zones as a standard. In Westport master baths where a large format tile is being installed on a shower floor, the membrane has to be set precisely so the slope to the drain is maintained across the full tile field without lippage. We do not rush the waterproofing and slope-setting phase because fixing a drainage problem through finished tile is a full rebuild.

Finish work covers floor and wall tile, vanity and countertop installation, all plumbing fixture connections, electrical updates for lighting and GFCI outlets, and exhaust fan installation. Westport bathroom projects frequently include radiant floor heating, freestanding tub placement with a floor-mounted supply valve, and custom shower niches built into the wall framing during rough-in. Permit coordination and a room-by-room final walkthrough are included in every Westport project.

data.title: What Makes Bathroom Remodeling in Westport Different
data.content:
Westport homeowners want bathrooms that make a visual statement , large-format tile, floating vanities, matte black or brushed gold fixtures, and frameless shower enclosures are common requests. Many Westport homes feature master suites where the bathroom connects to a walk-in closet, requiring careful moisture management and ventilation.

Coastal proximity in Compo Beach and Saugatuck means elevated humidity that affects material durability. We specify moisture-resistant materials and enhanced ventilation in all Westport bathrooms near the shore.

data.title: Westport Building Department and Permits
data.content: The Westport Building Department requires permits for bathroom renovations involving plumbing, electrical, or structural modifications. Coastal zone properties may have additional requirements. Review times typically run 2-3 weeks. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection.

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling Cost in Westport, CT
data.subtitle: Bathroom remodeling costs in Westport depend on the scope of work, material selections, and the condition of existing plumbing and structure. Ranges are illustrative for typical Connecticut projects. Final pricing depends on scope, materials, site conditions, and permit fees. Detailed written estimate provided before any work begins.

data.title: Why Bathroom Remodeling in Westport Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
Westport homes present bathroom conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

Westport bathrooms built before 2000 frequently have original plumbing that cannot support modern fixture configurations , low water pressure, undersized supply lines, and aging drain systems that need updating during a renovation.

Mid-century modern homes in Westport present unique conditions , slab-on-grade construction in some areas, radiant heat systems, and open floor plans that connect the bathroom to the bedroom without traditional wall separation. We know how to work within these architectural constraints.

data.title: Our Bathroom Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every bathroom remodel follows the same five-step process , structured to keep projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We visit your home, assess the existing bathroom conditions , plumbing, electrical, ventilation, structural , and discuss your goals. No charge. No obligation. We identify potential issues upfront so there are no surprises during construction.

steps.title: Planning
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering every trade: tile, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, vanity, fixtures, glass, and paint. Each line item is broken out so you understand exactly where the budget is going. We include a complete project schedule.

steps.title: Selections
steps.description: We guide you through tile, vanity, fixture, and hardware selections with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so everything arrives before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. Demolition, rough plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity installation, fixture connections, and paint , all sequenced properly. You receive daily updates and a clean, dust-controlled workspace.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished bathroom together , every tile, every grout line, every fixture, every piece of hardware. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks from demolition to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1–2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, material selections, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Material Lead Time
items.description: 1–4 Weeks. Vanity, tile, fixtures, and glass enclosure ordering and delivery.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 2–4 Weeks. Demo, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity, fixtures, and paint.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 2–3 Days. Hardware, accessories, glass installation, cleanup, and walkthrough.

items.question: How long does a bathroom remodel take in Westport?
items.answer: Most Westport bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks of active construction, plus material lead time. A hall bath typically takes 3-4 weeks. A full master bath remodel takes 5-8 weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule that accounts for all lead times.

items.question: How much does bathroom remodeling cost in Westport?
items.answer: Bathroom remodeling in Westport ranges from $8,000 for a half-bath update to $100,000+ for a upscale master suite with upgraded stone, radiant heat, and custom features. A standard hall bath remodel typically falls between $15,000 and $30,000. A master bath with walk-in shower and double vanity runs $30,000 to $60,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade.

items.question: Do I need a permit for bathroom remodeling in Westport?
items.answer: Yes. Most bathroom remodeling projects in Westport require permits from the Westport Building Department, particularly for plumbing and electrical work. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project.

items.question: What types of bathrooms do you remodel in Westport?
items.answer: We handle all bathroom types in Westport: half baths, hall baths, master baths, guest baths, and basement bathrooms. Westport's housing stock includes colonials, mid-century moderns, and contemporary homes. We're familiar with the construction conditions in these homes and account for them in every proposal.

items.question: Can you convert my tub to a walk-in shower in Westport?
items.answer: Yes. Tub-to-shower conversions are one of our most requested bathroom renovations in Westport. The project typically involves removing the existing tub, modifying the drain location, installing waterproofing membrane, and building a tiled walk-in shower with glass enclosure. Most conversions take 2-3 weeks and cost between $12,000 and $25,000 depending on tile selections and shower configuration.
